claimText: The Synchronous All India Population Estimation of Elephants (SAIEE) 2021-25 is India's first DNA-based elephant count, conducted by the Wildlife Institute of India (WII).
evidenceExcerpt: The Synchronous All India Population Estimation of Elephants (SAIEE) 2021-25 is India's first DNA-based count conducted by WII under Project Elephant.
examUse: Statement-based question on the methodology and executing agency of the latest elephant census.
distractorHooks: Conducted by the World Wildlife Fund (WWF)

claimText: India harbors approximately 60% of the global wild Asian Elephant population.
evidenceExcerpt: India harbors about 60% of the global Asian Elephant wild population (>22,000).
examUse: Statement-based question on India's share of the global Asian Elephant population.
distractorHooks: India harbors 30% of the global population
India harbors 90% of the global population
Refers to the global African Elephant population

claimText: The Western Ghats region hosts the largest population of wild elephants in India, followed by the North Eastern Hills.
evidenceExcerpt: The Western Ghats hosts the largest population, followed by North Eastern Hills.
examUse: Regional comparison question regarding elephant distribution in India.
distractorHooks: North Eastern Hills hosts the largest population
Himalayan foothills hosts the largest population

claimText: Karnataka has the highest state-wise elephant population in India, followed by Assam, Tamil Nadu, and Kerala.
evidenceExcerpt: State-wise, Karnataka supports the highest population, followed by Assam, Tamil Nadu, and Kerala.
examUse: Descending order or correct-pair question on state-wise elephant populations.
distractorHooks: Assam has the highest state-wise population
Kerala has the highest state-wise population

claimText: A small feral population of elephants exists in the Andaman Islands.
evidenceExcerpt: A small feral population of elephants exists in the Andaman Islands.
examUse: Statement-based question on the geographical distribution and presence of feral elephants in India.
distractorHooks: No elephants exist in the Andaman Islands
A feral population of elephants exists in Lakshadweep
